conversion: Current main-namespace members of Category:Hittite lemmas fetched through the MediaWiki API; Hittite lexical headings, head-template tr= transliterations and top-level English senses extracted by scripts/import-wiktionary-ancient.mjs. Slash- or bracket-delimited IPA found inside tr= is separated into pron before wikitext cleanup. Every entry retains its exact source revision. Community-edited coverage; not a comprehensive scholarly Hittite dictionary.

entries.def: suffix forming action nouns from verbal roots
Forms factitive verbs from noun stems
forms action and result nouns from verbs
Forms feminine nouns from common stems
Derives verbs from adjective stems meaning to become such adjective
Used to form fientive verbs from adjective stems meaning to become such adjective
Used to form causative verbs from verbs or adjectives
Forms possessive adjectives from nominal stems
Forms action nouns from verbal stems
Forms action nouns from verbs stems; Forms denominal abstract nouns
to be loved; to be good
to study an oracle, ask an oracle
watchtower, lookout, guardpost; fort, stronghold; watch, guard
a kind of grain; a kind of soup
to get lost, lose oneself; to disappear; to perish
to hold, have, keep; (auxiliary, with neuter participles) periphrastic perfect, equivalent to English "to have...-ed"
(transitive, active) to bash; (intransitive, middle) to crack
a bowl for liquids, employed in various rituals
like, such; in the manner of
eye; hole (of a sieve or needle?)
to soothe, to satiate, to satisfy
road, path, trail, route; (figuratively) way, behaviour; campaign, military expedition; journey, trip, mission; caravan, transport; occasion, occurrence, time
to quake, shake; to shiver, tremble
to happen, to occur, defend; to turn out to be
to cut, cut up; to amputate; to mutilate
an animal whose meat was dried and eaten, possibly a fish
skin, hide, fleece; skin-bag; also divinized as a fetish or talisman
to name, to summon, to call
to swear, to take an oath
oathtaking, swearing, oath; false oath, perjury
man, as determiner for male professionals
weighty; important, high-ranking, honored, esteemed; difficult, hard to reach, climb or traverse
fire; torch, torches; campfire, watchfire; embers, burning coals; fever, inflammation, burning, pain
to protect, guard, defend; to observe (agreements), keep (oaths), obey (commands), keep (a secret); (middle with dative) to seek protection with
to blow (a horn); to blow on, fan (fire or burning materials); to blow up, inflate
Uncertain meaning - something harmful, most likely a stick
to give; to pay; to grant; to hand over
(transitive) to raise, lift, elevate; to remove, take away; (intransitive) to rise, grow, go up
to throw, cast, shove; to abandon; to cast off, reject, discard; to ignore, disregard, neglect, forget
to run, race; to flee; to fly
to know; to be aware of; to experience; to heed, take note of, pay attention to; to recognize, acknowledge, accept; to remember
to seek out, give a sign, track
custom, rule, law; ceremony, rite; privilege, right
sign, omen, miracle(?); warning, feature, characteristic
apple, apricot?; (with determinative 𒄑) apple tree
possibly to embroider; possibly to sew; possibly to sew on; possibly to truss
big, great, large; preeminent, important; vast, spacious
to sleep; to rest, lie down

to impress (with a seal), to seal; to put on (headgear); to pitch (a tent); to prick, puncture, sting; to propel, shoot, hurl, throw; to squirt, spurt, flow; to shoot out, spring out, sprout forth; to press
Awarna, an ancient city identified with later Lycian Xanthus
Hatti, the region of Bronze Age Anatolia inhabited by the Hattians and then the Hittites
to perform a round dance, to dance in twisting manner
A god of Hayaša, listed in a fragmentary treaty between Hatti and Hayaša (KUB XXVI 39)
to step, go stand; to place oneself
to speak about; to conjure; to bewitch
word, speech; story; reason; matter, afair
something negative? Possibly calling them a "pack of wolves"
